content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed branch feat/t7j-drop-gitignored-from-checkpoint against origin/master. The implementation of bead t7j is correct and complete.

Part A (worktree.rs): build_checkpoint_tree now filters tracked-but-gitignored and assume-unchanged paths from the temporary checkpoint index before write-tree via three new helpers: tracked_gitignored_paths uses `ls-files -z -i -c --exclude-standard` scoped to the temp index; assume_unchanged_paths uses `ls-files -z -s -v` against the real repo index and parses lowercase status tags to find assume-unchanged entries (the assume-unchanged flag doesn't transfer via read-tree, so reading the real index is correct); remove_paths_from_index calls `update-index --force-remove` against the temp index. BTreeSet deduplicates the two lists before removal. Tab-in-filename edge case in the `-s -v` parser (split on \t and take nth(1)) would misparse paths containing tabs, but that is a non-realistic case and the downstream `update-index --force-remove` on the partial path is idempotent/harmless (verified: `update-index --force-remove <nonexistent>` exits 0).

Part B (commit 70eabf73): `git rm --cached result` untracks the symlink on this branch without deleting the physical file. Post-merge this leaves master clean — `git ls-files -i -c --exclude-standard` on the current checkout returns empty, satisfying the acceptance criterion.

Tests: Six new tests cover (a) tracked+gitignored, (b) reset-to-checkpoint survival after rm --cached, (c) assume-unchanged, (d) .git/info/exclude, (e) core.excludesFile, (f) non-UTF-8 paths (Unix-only). All checkpoint tests pass under cargo test --locked --features test-stub, and cargo fmt --check and cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings both pass. The one unrelated unit failure (workflow_engine_test::pre_commit_failure_remediation_survives_resume) pre-exists on master (ca4d2341) and is not caused by this branch.

The design decision to drop assume-unchanged paths entirely from the tree (rather than preserve the parent version) is explicitly what the bead specifies and is codified by the assume-unchanged test, so it is not a defect. No real bugs, correctness gaps, or safety problems found.

## Amendments

None.
